name: Arments - Walworth
description:
Arments has been serving authentic pie, mash and eels to London locals since 1914 and even have been awarded a prestigous blue plaque to celebrate this. 
They have been using the same recipe  during this time that keeps people coming through their doors every day.

name: Arthur's - Great Yarmouth
description:
Arthurs, serving tradional pie and mash to the people of Great Yarmouth.
Pies are supplied by F Cooke's in Bishops Stortford with the mash and liqour made on site by the owner Matt.

name: Barney's - Walthamstow
description: Opened in 2018 Barney's is run by Tommy who has been dishing up quality pie, mash and liquor to the locals of Wallthamstow and those futher afield since he had enough of the plumbing business.

name: Bert's - Wickford
description: Bert's pie and mash at the Duke Public House
address_line1: The Duke Public House, High Street

name: BJ's - Canning Town
description:
Owned by Nathan, a member of the Jacobie family, who have been in the trade for 50 years.
Pie, mash and liquor made fresh on the premises every day.

name: Spudsy Malone - Southend
description: Previously run under the name 'Brickies' by ex-bracklayer Jonny Malone. Everything freshly cooked on the premises every day.

name: Brooke - Dagenham
description: A traditional East End pie, mash and ell house serving the locals of Dagenham for over 20 years.

name: Bush - Shepherds Bush
description: Bush pie, mash and liquor is a family run business serving freshly made fayre on the premises.

name: Castles - Camden
description: Founded in 1920 by Lil Castle, a short walk from Camden Market.

name: Cathedral Pie Shop - Rochester
description: Lovely little shop in this historic city.  Pie, mash and liquor made fresh on the premises every day.

name: Champs - Poole
description:
Champs Traditional Pie and Mash was opened in early 2025 by Warren Roberts and Megan Hogarth, who moved from London to Poole and realized the area was missing a "proper" pie shop.
Pies sourced from Manzes , London and cooked on site along with mash and liquor made fresh on premises.

name: Chestnut Pie & Mash - South Ockenden
description: Chestnut Pie & Mash is a relative new addition to the local food scene, located within a garden centre. Pies supplied by Maureens.

name: Cockneys - Croydon
description:
Cockneys in Croydon is owned and run by Max who has been working in the Pie and Mash business since the age of 14.
It has been serving up traditional fayre to the people of Croyon everyday for over 35 years.

name: Cockneys - Portobello Road
description: A family run business of over 37 years run by Ruth and her son Bill. They prepare and freshly bake traditonal Pie and Mash on their premises.

name: Covs - Clacton
description: Covs in Claction is run by the passionate Matthew and Lisa.  Pies, mash and liquour are prepared daily.

name: Eastenders - Chrisp Street Market
description: Run with passion and dedication by Brian and his son David. They serve fresh pie, mash and liquour in the heart of the East End.

name: F Cooke - Bishops Stortford
description: F Cooke's is a family name etched firmly in the history of Pie and Mash. The owner, Fred Cooke, is a 5th generaion purveyor of Pie and Mash, now to the local and vistors to hes shop in Bishops Stortford.

name: M Manze - Tower Bridge Road
description: One of the oldest pie and mash shops in London serving traditional pie mash and liquor since 1902.
